LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE AUG-22-2018 HEARING ON DEFENDANT WILLIAM TAYLOR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST HIM ON APRIL 17, 2017 AND THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST HIM ON APRIL 22, 2018, THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 6, 2018 TO GIVE MR. TAYLOR AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE A PROPOSED ANSWER WHICH IT APPEARS HE INADVERTENTLY FAILED TO DO. MR. TAYLOR HAS UNTIL AUGUST 28, 2018 TO FILE A PROPOSED ANSWER AND, IF HE DOES, PLAINTIFF CACH, LLC HAS UNTIL SEPTEMBER 4, 2018 TO FILE ANY OBJECTION TO THAT PROPOSED ANSWER. ASSUMING THAT MR. TAYLOR FILES A PROPOSED ANSWER IN PROPER FORM, THE COURT INTENDS TO GRANT MR. TAYLOR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND DENY THE MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. CACH HAS NOT SUBMITTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO REFUTE MR. TAYLOR'S STATEMENT UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY THAT HE FIRST LEARNED OF THIS LAWSUIT ON OR AROUND JULY 16, 2018 AND THAT HE WAS NOT EVADING SERVICE. THOSE STATEMENTS ARE SUFFICIENT TO WARRANT SETTING ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-04-2018 Set For Trial On Feb-25-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Defualt Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT WILLIAM TAYLOR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant William Taylor's motion to set aside the default entered against him on April 17, 2017 and the default judgment entered against him on April 22, 2018 is granted. The default and default judgment are set aside. Mr. Taylor's additional motion to quash service of summons is denied. CACH has not submitted sufficient evidence to refute Mr. Taylor's statement under penalty of perjury that he first learned of this lawsuit on or around July 16, 2018 and that he was not evading service. Those statements are sufficient to warrant setting aside the default and default judgment. Mr. Taylor must file his answer no later than September 14, 2018. Mr. Taylor has not shown that the service of summons should be quashed. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

*After the matter was called, Defendant Willaim Taylor appeared and was advised of the Court's ruling in open court, off the record.*

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Defualt Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-22-2018 Continued To Sep-06-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
William Taylor, Defendant and self-represented litigant, 415.566.8059, appearing in pro per;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

There being no other appearance, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant William Taylor's motion to set aside the default entered against him on April 17, 2017 and the default judgment entered against him on April 22, 2018 is continued to September 6, 2018 to give Mr. Taylor an opportunity to file a proposed answer which it appears he inadvertently failed to do. Mr. Taylor has until August 28, 2018 to file a proposed answer and, if he does, plaintiff CACH, LLC has until September 4, 2018 to file any objection to that proposed answer. Assuming that Mr. Taylor files a proposed answer in proper form, the court intends to grant Mr. Taylor's motion to set aside the default and default judgment and deny the motion to quash service of summons. CACH has not submitted sufficient evidence to refute Mr. Taylor's statement under penalty of perjury that he first learned of this lawsuit on or around July 16, 2018 and that he was not evading service. Those statements are sufficient to warrant setting aside the default and default judgment.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)
